A wonderful experience. Great staff at the visitor centre and amazing views. Do not miss this. Aim to arrive at the bridge for sunset. Be prepared for a long walk but it is worth it.
Lands End offers stunning coastal trails with breathtaking views of the Golden Gate Bridge and the Pacific Ocean.

This is a very interesting walking/hiking area very close to the city.  We walked on a very foggy/rainy day and so the visibility was close to nothing.  There is a light house off the coast, and we didn't even see it - just the light flashing in the fog.  I am told on a clear day you get a great view of the Golden Gate bridge.   We walked down to 'Mile Rock Beach' from the main trail.  Note that there are 255 steps and when you go down to the beach, you will need to climb back up!   We also went to see the labyrinth that I was told was there (and it still shows up on the map), but it was removed.  We also saw a coyote just off the trail, which was quite interesting!  (So be on the look out - and perhaps don't let small dogs off their leash.)  All in all, it was a nice walk and we would definetely come back and do this again, hopefully on a clear day.
